plan_review_notes: Approved scope: bootstrap the task worktree onto the local evolution foundation, then implement a bounded Sisyphus-backed harness executor shape plus focused tests without taking on MCP surface or promotion flow.

plan_review_history.notes: Concrete slice: first align the new task worktree to the current local evolution foundation, because repo main is still behind the evolution package baseline. Then extend harness.py from summary-only default metrics into a Sisyphus-backed execution evaluator shape that can create isolated evaluation evidence and real execution hooks while preserving live-state isolation. Keep scope to harness execution and tests; defer MCP surface and promotion flow.

Approved scope: bootstrap the task worktree onto the local evolution foundation, then implement a bounded Sisyphus-backed harness executor shape plus focused tests without taking on MCP surface or promotion flow.

spec_review_notes: Freeze scope to harness execution only. The task may align its worktree to the current local evolution baseline, implement a Sisyphus-backed evaluation executor shape in harness.py, update runner integration as needed, and add focused tests. Exclude MCP UI surface, mutator generation, and promotion/PR materialization.

normal_cases.name: Harness execution can produce completed evaluation metrics through the summary fallback path and through a Sisyphus-backed evaluation evidence path.

edge_cases.name: Narrowed candidate scope and non-auto-executed Sisyphus evaluation requests still produce stable harness and runner results.

exception_cases.name: Sisyphus evaluation or guarded follow-up launch failures surface actionable failure metadata, including isolated task evidence when available.

verification_methods.target: Harness execution can produce completed evaluation metrics through the summary fallback path and through a Sisyphus-backed evaluation evidence path.
Narrowed candidate scope and non-auto-executed Sisyphus evaluation requests still produce stable harness and runner results.
Sisyphus evaluation or guarded follow-up launch failures surface actionable failure metadata, including isolated task evidence when available.
